methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $32M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Planted Solar is a Series A-stage climate and energy company that has raised $32M to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes 9 peer companies (1 public, 8 private), with notable comparable firms including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

The valuation analysis applies a stage-progression methodology, the only method with sufficient data support. Using total capital raised ($32M) as a base proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression, this method implies a valuation of $64M. A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the Climate & Energy sector (fewer than the three datapoints required for statistical reliability).

The triangulated valuation range is $54M to $74M, with a median estimate of $64M. This relatively narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodology. Key uncertainties include: the absence of tighter peer comparables at Planted Solar's stage and valuation tier, which limits external validation; the appropriateness of the 2.0x stage multiplier to this specific company's growth trajectory and market conditions; and the quality of total-raised-as-proxy in the absence of a documented last-round valuation. A future funding round or emergence of additional near-stage comparables would materially strengthen conviction in this range.
